microwave_time.user_tendencies: IF eating alone in a quiet personal setting AND neutral AND refrigerated savory meal THEN 2 min BECAUSE they likely want food warm without excessive delay. IF eating alone in a quiet personal setting AND physically fatigued AND refrigerated savory meal THEN 3 min BECAUSE fatigue increases desire for comfort and a hotter meal. IF eating alone in a quiet personal setting AND in a hurry AND refrigerated savory meal THEN 1 min BECAUSE efficiency outweighs maximizing warmth. IF any dining context AND neutral AND fruit or cold dessert THEN no microwave BECAUSE those foods are intended to be served cold. IF social setting with a person to either side AND relaxed with ample time AND refrigerated savory meal THEN 2 min BECAUSE they still want warm food but do not prioritize extra heating. IF watching television or working on a device in front AND physically fatigued AND refrigerated savory meal THEN 3 min BECAUSE comfort takes priority when attention is divided. IF social setting AND in a hurry AND refrigerated savory meal THEN 1 min BECAUSE time pressure dominates over ideal serving temperature.

occlusion_relevance.user_tendencies: IF eating alone in a quiet personal setting AND neutral AND any food without special visual demands THEN do not consider occlusion BECAUSE there is no important visual target to protect. IF eating while watching television in front AND neutral AND any food THEN minimize front occlusion BECAUSE preserving the screen view matters. IF eating while working on a laptop or tablet in front AND neutral AND any food THEN minimize front occlusion BECAUSE the user needs to see the device continuously. IF social setting with a person to the left AND neutral AND any food THEN minimize left occlusion BECAUSE the user likely wants to maintain sightline to the partner. IF social setting with a person to the right AND neutral AND any food THEN minimize right occlusion BECAUSE the user likely wants to maintain sightline to the partner. IF watching television in front AND physically fatigued AND any food THEN minimize front occlusion BECAUSE fatigue increases reliance on passive viewing and this outweighs speed. IF social setting on one side AND in a hurry AND any food THEN minimize left occlusion or minimize right occlusion accordingly BECAUSE this user consistently prioritizes preserving the active social sightline over small efficiency gains.

robot_speed.user_tendencies: IF eating alone in a quiet personal setting AND neutral AND any food THEN medium BECAUSE it balances comfort and efficiency. IF eating alone in a quiet personal setting AND physically fatigued AND any food THEN slow BECAUSE fatigue increases sensitivity to motion near the face. IF any dining context AND in a hurry AND any food THEN fast BECAUSE this user accepts more automation and speed when time pressure is high. IF social setting with a person to either side AND neutral AND any food THEN medium BECAUSE moderate speed feels socially acceptable without making the meal too long. IF social setting with a person to either side AND physically fatigued AND any food THEN slow BECAUSE fatigue tie-breaks over social efficiency for safety and comfort. IF watching television or working on a device in front AND neutral AND any food THEN medium BECAUSE predictable movement is preferred while attention is divided. IF watching television or working on a device in front AND in a hurry AND any food THEN fast BECAUSE time pressure dominates when the user is already multitasking.

skewering_axis.user_tendencies: IF eating alone in a quiet personal setting AND neutral AND bite-sized solid food such as meat or fruit THEN perpendicular to major axis BECAUSE it improves acquisition success. IF any dining context AND neutral AND elongated or narrow food that could create an overly wide bite THEN parallel to major axis BECAUSE narrower bites are easier to manage. IF social setting AND neutral AND foods prone to failed pickup THEN perpendicular to major axis BECAUSE avoiding visible retries matters more than bite narrowness. IF any dining context AND physically fatigued AND elongated food THEN parallel to major axis BECAUSE easier oral handling matters more when tired. IF any dining context AND in a hurry AND foods prone to slipping or breakup THEN perpendicular to major axis BECAUSE success and speed are prioritized. IF watching television or working on a device in front AND neutral AND ordinary solid foods THEN perpendicular to major axis BECAUSE the user prefers fewer interruptions from acquisition failures. IF social setting AND physically fatigued AND elongated food THEN parallel to major axis BECAUSE this user’s tie-break policy under fatigue is to reduce eating effort even if acquisition is slightly less robust.

web_interface_confirmation.user_tendencies: IF eating alone in a quiet personal setting AND neutral AND easy-to-acquire foods THEN no BECAUSE the user prefers streamlined interaction. IF social setting with a person to either side AND neutral AND foods prone to failed pickup THEN yes BECAUSE explicit confirmation avoids awkward empty-fork transfers. IF any dining context AND physically fatigued AND any food THEN no BECAUSE fatigue makes extra interface steps undesirable. IF working on a laptop or tablet in front AND neutral AND any food THEN no BECAUSE switching attention to confirm is disruptive. IF watching television in front AND neutral AND any food THEN no BECAUSE the user prefers low interaction burden during passive viewing. IF social setting AND in a hurry AND easy-to-acquire foods THEN no BECAUSE speed dominates when failure risk is low. IF social setting AND relaxed with ample time AND messy or fragile foods THEN yes BECAUSE the user is willing to trade time for reliability and social smoothness.

transfer_mode.user_tendencies: IF eating alone in a quiet personal setting AND neutral AND any food or drink THEN inside mouth transfer BECAUSE the user cannot lean forward due to poor trunk control. IF any dining context AND physically fatigued AND any food or drink THEN inside mouth transfer BECAUSE fatigue further reduces any ability to reach outward. IF social setting with a person to either side AND neutral AND any food or drink THEN inside mouth transfer BECAUSE physical necessity outweighs social modesty. IF watching television or working on a device in front AND neutral AND any food or drink THEN inside mouth transfer BECAUSE it minimizes the need for user movement while attention is divided. IF any dining context AND in a hurry AND any food or drink THEN inside mouth transfer BECAUSE it is the most direct and physically compatible mode. IF any dining context AND relaxed with ample time AND any food or drink THEN inside mouth transfer BECAUSE extra time does not change the user’s inability to lean forward. IF social setting AND physically fatigued AND any food or drink THEN inside mouth transfer BECAUSE the tie-break policy always favors the physically feasible low-effort option.

outside_mouth_distance.user_tendencies: IF eating alone in a quiet personal setting AND neutral AND any food or drink THEN not applicable BECAUSE the default transfer mode is inside mouth transfer. IF any dining context AND physically fatigued AND any food or drink THEN not applicable BECAUSE fatigue does not make outside-mouth transfer feasible. IF social setting with a person to either side AND neutral AND any food or drink THEN not applicable BECAUSE social context cannot override trunk-control limitations. IF watching television or working on a device in front AND neutral AND any food or drink THEN not applicable BECAUSE the user still requires inside-mouth transfer. IF any dining context AND in a hurry AND any food or drink THEN not applicable BECAUSE the most efficient feasible mode remains inside-mouth transfer. IF any dining context AND relaxed with ample time AND any food or drink THEN not applicable BECAUSE additional time does not change physical compatibility. IF any dining context AND any affective state AND any food condition THEN not applicable BECAUSE this field only applies to outside-mouth transfer, which this user does not prefer.

convey_robot_ready_for_initiating_transf.user_tendencies: IF eating alone in a quiet personal setting AND neutral AND any food THEN speech + LED BECAUSE multimodal cues are clear and reduce uncertainty. IF social setting with a person to either side AND neutral AND any food THEN LED BECAUSE it is less socially intrusive than speech. IF social setting with a person to either side AND physically fatigued AND any food THEN speech + LED BECAUSE fatigue tie-breaks toward maximum clarity. IF any dining context AND in a hurry AND any food THEN LED BECAUSE the user wants concise signaling with minimal delay. IF eating alone in a quiet personal setting AND relaxed with ample time AND any food THEN speech + LED BECAUSE richer feedback feels comfortable when efficiency pressure is low.

detect_user_ready_for_initiating_transfe.user_tendencies: IF eating alone in a quiet personal setting AND neutral AND any food THEN open mouth BECAUSE it gives user control without requiring arm use. IF social setting with a person to either side AND neutral AND any food THEN autocontinue BECAUSE open-mouth detection may be confused with talking and buttons are impossible. IF social setting with a person to either side AND physically fatigued AND any food THEN autocontinue BECAUSE fatigue reinforces the low-effort option. IF watching television in front AND neutral AND any food THEN open mouth BECAUSE the user can intentionally signal readiness while keeping hands free. IF working on a laptop or tablet in front AND neutral AND any food THEN autocontinue BECAUSE divided attention makes explicit mouth signaling less convenient. IF any dining context AND in a hurry AND any food THEN autocontinue BECAUSE the user accepts more automation to reduce meal time. IF eating alone in a quiet personal setting AND neutral AND any drink THEN open mouth BECAUSE it provides simple hands-free control. IF working on a laptop or tablet in front AND neutral AND any drink THEN autocontinue BECAUSE multitasking makes explicit signaling less desirable. retract_between_bites.user_tendencies: IF eating alone in a quiet personal setting AND neutral AND any food THEN no BECAUSE the user prefers efficiency when no important view is being blocked. IF watching television in front AND neutral AND any food THEN yes BECAUSE preserving the front view matters. bite_dipping_preference.user_tendencies: IF eating alone in a quiet personal setting AND neutral AND no sauce or no dippable food THEN do not dip BECAUSE dipping is not relevant. IF eating alone in a quiet personal setting AND neutral AND sauce is available for a compatible food THEN less BECAUSE the user likely wants flavor without increasing mess. IF eating alone in a quiet personal setting AND relaxed with ample time AND sauce is available THEN more BECAUSE there is less concern about mess or speed. IF any social setting AND neutral AND sauce is available THEN less BECAUSE the user wants to avoid messy transfers in front of others. IF any social setting AND in a hurry AND sauce is available THEN do not dip BECAUSE efficiency and cleanliness both outweigh extra flavor. IF any dining context AND physically fatigued AND sauce is available THEN less BECAUSE some flavor is desirable but excessive sauce may complicate transfer. IF watching television or working on a device in front AND neutral AND sauce is available THEN less BECAUSE the user prefers lower mess while attention is divided.

wait_before_autocontinue_seconds.user_tendencies: IF eating alone in a quiet personal setting AND neutral AND any food or drink or wiping THEN 100 sec BECAUSE it balances autonomy with enough time to intervene via mouth or head cues. IF any dining context AND in a hurry AND any task THEN 10 sec BECAUSE the user wants the system to keep moving. IF social setting with a person to either side AND neutral AND any task THEN 100 sec BECAUSE some extra time helps manage conversation without making the system feel stalled. IF social setting with a person to either side AND physically fatigued AND any task THEN 10 sec BECAUSE this user’s tie-break policy under fatigue favors reduced effort and more automation. IF watching television in front AND neutral AND any task THEN 100 sec BECAUSE the user may be distracted but does not want extremely long pauses. IF working on a laptop or tablet in front AND neutral AND any task THEN 100 sec BECAUSE moderate waiting accommodates divided attention. IF relaxed with ample time in any context AND any task THEN 1000 sec BECAUSE the user is comfortable with long pauses when not rushed and may want maximal opportunity to intervene.
